title: Etsy shares pop on Q1 results, improved outlook
text: Etsy shares popped following the release of Q1 2026 earnings results and improved outlook. The company beat revenue expectations with $631.3 million in revenue against forecasts of $620.9 million. Gross merchandise sales on Etsy's core marketplace reached $2.5 billion, up 5.5% year-over-year, marking the second consecutive quarter of marketplace GMS growth. Active buyers grew sequentially for the first time in two years, demonstrating a turnaround in buyer engagement metrics. GMS per active buyer reached $122 on a trailing twelve-month basis, rising year-over-year for the first time since late 2022 and marking the fourth straight quarter of sequential improvement. Mobile app GMS grew 11.2% year-over-year, with app transactions accounting for about 47% of total GMS. The company maintained its full-year adjusted EBITDA margin outlook of 28% to 30% and said it expects year-over-year GMS growth in each quarter of 2026. The improved guidance and strong app growth drove investor optimism about Etsy's AI-driven growth initiatives and marketplace focus.
